HomePage.aiTitle: If you want to ask AI about me:
HomePage.bio: To put it briefly, I am someone who takes security seriously but finds fun in the details.
HomePage.checkMenu: If you wonder what I do, you can check the menus above; if you are on mobile, you can check the menus from the 3 lines at the top right.

Footer.slogan: I protect with code, I tell stories with the lens.

ContactPage.reachOut: Feel free to reach out for collaborations or just to say hi.

AboutPage.title: About Me
AboutPage.intro: Hello! I'm Ege, 22 years old. I was born on July 11, 2003, in Lüleburgaz, Kırklareli. I am from the last generation that grew up playing ball on the street and clashing cards and pogs. I met my first computer in 2011; since that day, technology has become an inseparable part of my life. My interest in technology deepened with university. Respectively;
AboutPage.hobbies: In my spare time, I like taking photos, editing them in Lightroom, listening to music, and playing games. I am a huge Formula 1 and Fenerbahçe fan. I also love exploring cities and visiting cultural areas like museums and ruins.
AboutPage.summary: In short, I love dealing with technology, creating things, and learning something new every day.

PhotographyPage.title: Photography
PhotographyPage.intro: Yes, if you read the about section, you now know who I am. If you haven't read it, you can go back and read it <link>here</link>. Now I can tell you my story about photography.
PhotographyPage.meaningTitle: What does photography mean to me?
PhotographyPage.meaningContent: Photography is somewhat of an escape from the screen for me. Usually, my day passes in front of the computer. That's why taking my camera, going out, wandering around, and photographing things really does me good. I enjoy transforming the details I see with my eyes into a frame and capturing the moment.
PhotographyPage.startContent: I bought my first camera, a Canon Rebel T7 and 18-55mm lens, on November 8, 2023. After learning the basics of photography with this camera, I gained experience using 75-300mm and 50mm lenses in addition to the 18-55mm until I bought my new camera. Then I sold everything I had and bought a Sony A7M2 and 28-75mm lens. After using this camera for about 2.5 months, I decided to buy the Fujifilm XM-5, which I own after the Sony A7M2. I am adding the list of equipment I currently have below with links; you can click on them to learn the details:

equipment.name: Fujinon XC 15-45mm f/3.5-5.6 OIS PZ Lens

CyberPage.title: Cybersecurity
CyberPage.introContent: Cybersecurity is not just an area of interest for me, but a disciplined learning and architectural design process. I focus on understanding systems in depth, detecting vulnerabilities, and building structures based on the "Zero Trust" principle.
CyberPage.osIntro: I have gained experience with a wide range of operating systems to analyze different system architectures and behaviors.
osPast: Security Focused: Kali Linux, Parrot OS
Server & Desktop: CentOS, Fedora, Linux Mint, Windows 10 / 7 / Vista
CyberPage.techIntro: Beyond standard installations, I manage a Hybrid Architecture that balances performance and security.

techItems.title: 1. Hybrid Server Management (Docker & Native)
techItems.desc: Instead of confining all services to a single structure, I use a distribution optimized according to needs:
list: Micro-Services: I run database, automation, and media services in isolated containers on Docker.
Web Applications: I host my projects like cv.egekaya.net and egekaya.net, where performance is critical, in a native environment with Next.js infrastructure.

techItems.title: 2. Cloudflare Supported Edge Security and Custom Logging
techItems.desc: I manually manage the DNS configurations (A, CNAME, MX, TXT) and email server security settings (SPF/DMARC) of my sites.
list: Cloudflare Tunnels: I use secure tunneling protocols to open my internal network to the outside world without the risk of opening ports.
Custom HTTP Traffic Analysis (Custom Logging): Not content with ready-made logging tools, I developed my own monitoring system using Cloudflare Workers.
How It Works? All requests to my sites are captured at the "Edge" point (before returning a response to the client).
Processing: Captured data (IP, Method, Path, Timestamp) is transmitted to my server via Webhook and archived locally. This way, I can monitor all traffic from a central point.

techItems.title: 3. SecOps and Automation (n8n & AI)
techItems.desc: I shifted security processes from manual operations to Event-Driven automations.
list: Continuous Discovery and Scanning: The cycle starts automatically every day with Nmap; the system is scanned end-to-end to map open ports and services.
Automated Vulnerability Analysis: Triggered after Nmap, Nuclei and OWASP ZAP perform in-depth vulnerability scans on detected services.
AI Supported Reporting: All results are read from the disk, analyzed by artificial intelligence agents, and critical findings are transmitted to me as instant notifications (Telegram/Email).

techItems.title: 4. Network Security and Access
list: Mesh VPN: I use Tailscale for access to my servers and services. In this way, I can access my systems in an encrypted way from anywhere with internet, as if I were on the local network.
DNS Filtering: I block ad and tracker traffic network-wide at the DNS level with Pi-hole.

CyberPage.experienceSummary: Currently, I am developing active projects on system management, web technologies, and security automations, and increasing my competencies on the Blue Team (Defense) side.
CyberPage.philosophy: "Security is not a product, but a process. I aim to improve this process at every step, from the logging systems I wrote myself to the network topology I established."

content.p: This policy describes the personal data that may be processed through the current implementation of this website. The site is primarily a content-focused personal website. It does not include user accounts, advertising technology, or analytics tooling in the current codebase.

If you browse this website, limited technical request data may be processed by the server or delivery infrastructure, such as IP address, request headers, and basic request metadata required to deliver and protect the service. If you use the contact form, the submitted name, email address, and message are processed. The contact flow also processes basic anti-abuse signals used to reduce spam and misuse.

The data processed through this website is used only for the operation of the site, delivery of requested content, handling contact form submissions, responding to messages, and protecting the service against abuse or excessive automated requests.

The current codebase does not intentionally implement analytics cookies, advertising cookies, behavioral profiling cookies, or an on-site cookie preference panel. No optional cookie or local storage feature is currently implemented in the website code. If strictly necessary technical storage or similar mechanisms are applied by the browser, framework, or hosting/runtime environment as part of delivering the site, they are limited to basic operation and security.

Contact form submissions are sent to the site owner's configured email inbox through the email delivery setup used by the site. Technical request data may also be processed by the hosting or network infrastructure required to serve the website. Aside from those operational needs and applicable legal obligations, this website is not designed to share visitor data for advertising or profiling purposes.

This repository does not implement a separate user database, user accounts, or profile management for visitors. Contact form submissions may be retained in the site owner's email inbox for as long as reasonably necessary to review and respond to the message.

Depending on the laws that apply to you, you may have rights regarding access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data, as well as the right to object to or restrict certain processing. Requests will be reviewed in light of the actual technical scope of this website and any applicable legal obligations.
For privacy-related questions about this website or requests concerning personal data submitted through the contact form, you can contact: iamegekaya@egekaya.net
This policy reflects the current implementation of the website and may be updated if the site's behavior changes. Last updated: July 16, 2025

This Cookie Policy describes the cookie-related behavior of the website as it is currently implemented. The current codebase does not intentionally include analytics, marketing, or preference-management cookies, and it does not include an on-site cookie consent or preference panel.

Based on the current website implementation, no optional analytics, behavioral, advertising, or social-plugin cookies are intentionally set by the site code. The website is primarily rendered as content pages with a contact form and localized routes.

Basic technical mechanisms may still be used by the browser, framework, or hosting/runtime environment when needed to deliver pages, handle requests, or protect the service. If such storage or cookie-like behavior occurs, it is limited to essential technical operation and security rather than profiling or marketing.
No local storage or similar browser-side preference storage is intentionally implemented in the current website code.

You can manage or block cookies through your browser settings. Because the site does not currently provide a cookie preference panel, browser controls are the primary way to manage cookie behavior on your device.

You can review your browser's help or privacy settings to manage cookies. Common examples include: Google Chrome: chrome://settings/cookies Mozilla Firefox: about:preferences#privacy Safari: Preferences > Privacy Microsoft Edge: edge://settings/privacy Opera: opera://settings/privacy

This website may contain links to external websites or services. If you follow those links, the third-party service may apply its own cookies or tracking technologies under its own policies. This Cookie Policy applies only to the behavior of this website as implemented in this repository.
This Cookie Policy may be updated if the implementation of the website changes. Last updated: July 16, 2025
